description:
President Donald Trump has identified the Iranian navy as a major target in the US campaign against the Islamic Republic of Iran. US forces have struck a range of port infrastructure and key vessels to degrade its capability, destroying more than 100 vessels by March 16.

title: Israeli strikes eliminate Iranian security chief Larijani and Basij commander Soleimani
description:
Overnight Israeli strikes in Iran killed Ali Larijani, the secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council, and Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani. The eliminations add to the growing list of top Iranian officials who have been killed by Israeli and American strikes since the conflict began on February 28.

description: US President Donald Trump on Monday walked back his own deadline for escalating attacks on Iran, citing "productive" talks with Tehran – but Iranian officials deny those talks took place. So what is really happening? Here is a look at the facts and speculation.

title: Middle East war weighs heavy on Iranian population
description: Getting a sense of the real death toll and the psychological toll of the war in the Middle East is a real challenge. According to estimates, at least 1,500 Iranians have died since the start of the US-Israeli military campaign. We spoke to a sociologist on how it is effecting civilians. Please be warned some of these images are disturbing.

title: EU and Australia sign free trade deal and boost defence cooperation
description: The European Union and Australia struck a long-awaited free-trade deal on March 24. The two countries have also agreed to boost defence cooperation and access to crucial rare-earth minerals in the face of global uncertainty over trade. Speaking while visiting Australia, EU chief Ursula von der Leyen said it was necessary at a time where tariffs and supplu chains are being weaponised.

title: US 'shifts' towards diplomacy with Iran ahead of midterms
description: Hopes rose that the Iran war could begin winding down as U.S. President Donald Trump said that his government was holding productive talks with Tehran. Although this was denied by Iran, it is a further sign that the US is turning towards diplomacy to end a conflict that could drag on. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle breaks it down for us.

title: Israeli forces to control south Lebanon up to Litani river
description: Israel said on March 24 that its military would take control of south Lebanon up to the Litani River, around 30 kilometres (20 miles) from the border, as deadly strikes pounded the country. The day before, Israel's finance minister had said Israel should annex the region. This comes as Israel continues to target Hezbollah targets in south Lebanon and southern suburbs of Beirut. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin breaks it down for us.

title: What do we know about the negotiations between the US and Iran?
description: It's day 25 of the US and Israel's war with Iran, and US President Donald Trump claims that "very good" talks are underway to end combat. He said that because of this, the US will hold off on striking Iranian power plants. But Iranian officials have contradicted this - downplaying Trump's threats and saying he's actually backing down.

title: In US, thousands of Cuban migrants who arrived legally fear deportation

title: Increase in hostilities between israel and Hezbollah shows 'no sign of slowing down'
description: An Israeli strike on Bshamoun, a town south of Beirut, killed at least two people. It also continued strikes on the southern suburbs of the capital. Since the start of Israeli strikes on the country, at least 1,039 people have been killed. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin is in Beirut, and tells us more from the ground.

title: EU, Australia seal free trade deal eight years in the making
description: The EU and Australia have signed a sweeping free trade agreement that will eliminate tariffs on nearly all trade between them as global economies seek to hedge against chaotic US tariff policies. Also in this edition: crude oil prices rise again as traders remain sceptical of signals that Washington is trying to de-escalate its war with Iran.

title: ICE agents start appearing in US airports amid shutdown chaos
description: The Trump administration has begun deploying ICE agents across several airports in the US. Officials say the agents are being sent to at least 14 airports - including hubs in New York, Chicago and Atlanta - to assist overstretched TSA staff during a government shutdown that has caused long lines at security checkpoints across the country.

title: EU signs trade deal with Australia as it looks for new partners amid tension with US
description: The European Union and Australia struck a long-awaited free-trade deal on March 24, while also agreeing to boost defence cooperation and access to crucial rare-earth minerals in the face of global uncertainty over trade. It's the latest Brussels has agreed to, to diversify trade. But the deal was slammed by EU farmers, who have called 'unacceptable'. FRANCE 24's Dave Keating has more from Brussels.

The post We are scared, terrified: London Jews on edge after antisemitic arson attack appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Ukraine strikes key Russian oil port and refinery
description: Ukraine said it hit a key Russian oil terminal at Primorsk and a refinery in Ufa on Monday.

title: What Trump said about Iran after postponing military strikes
description: After announcing the US will postpone strikes on Iran’s power plants, Donald Trump said Iran called him to make a deal.

title: The Billionaire Funding France’s Far Right
description: Pierre-Édouard Stérin is financing projects to make France less Muslim, more Catholic and more capitalist. He says his program has trained thousands running for municipal office on Sunday.

title: Trump Delays Energy Strikes, but Iran’s Infrastructure Is Already Battered
description: U.S. and Israeli attacks on power plants and other civilian infrastructure risk escalating the conflict across the region, and angering Iranians who oppose the government.

title: Four wounded as Hezbollah pounds north with rockets, IDF strikes in Beirut
description:
Victim hit in face by shrapnel as salvo reaches border town of Kiryat Shmona; IDF captures two Hezbollah fighters, blows up Litani River crossing; IRGC official targeted in Lebanese capital
The post Four wounded as Hezbollah pounds north with rockets, IDF strikes in Beirut appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Whose war is it? Trump eyes Iran talks as Israel hints at long campaign
description: Another deadline day, another ultimatum, another surprise turn from the US president. The same Donald Trump who may or may not send ground troops to try and secure the Strait of Hormuz now affirming that his administration's talking with the Iranians and thus holding off on hitting critical infrastructure. Trump's messaging since the start 24 days ago keeping friends and foes alike guessing. That's in stark contrast to Israeli allies, who in word

title: Tehran residents express distrust over Trump’s war diplomacy
description: “If we trust him, we lose.”

title: Iran’s parliament speaker Mohammed Qalibaf enters spotlight amid reported US talks
description:
A former Revolutionary Guards commander, Tehran mayor, police chief, presidential candidate and protégé of Ali Khamenei is key node between political, security and clerical elites
The post Irans parliament speaker Mohammed Qalibaf enters spotlight amid reported US talks appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Oil prices volatile after Trump's Strait of Hormuz threat
description: Oil prices remained well north of $100 per barrel after markets opened Sunday, with the Iran war moving into its fourth week.Why it matters: The price suggests that traders don't see a near-term end to the conflict, or risks to oil transit in the Strait of Hormuz that's throttling supplies on an unprecedented scale.It's the first major trading since President Trump said Saturday night that Iran has 48 hours to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or else

title: A web of sensors: How the US spots missiles and drones from Iran
description: If a missile is launched from Iran toward a U.S. military base in the region, how do service members know in time to stay safe?
